title: IDNYC — free municipal ID
description: Free photo ID accepted across NYC. Useful as a stepping stone while waiting on a state ID.
location: Multiple enrollment centers across all five boroughs
eligibility: Any NYC resident 10+. No conviction-related restrictions.

details.application_process: Book an appointment at nyc.gov/idnyc or call 311. Bring documents showing identity + residency (point system).

title: New York State non-driver ID
description: State-issued photo ID. Required for most employment, banking, and benefits.

details.what_to_bring: Proof of identity (birth certificate, Social Security card), proof of NY residency, application form MV-44. Fee waiver available with proof of public assistance.

title: SNAP food benefits
description: Monthly food assistance. Apply online via ACCESS HRA or at any HRA center.
eligibility: Income-based. Most adults released from incarceration are eligible.

details.application_process: access.nyc.gov → ACCESS HRA. Interview by phone after applying.

title: Medicaid health coverage
description: Free or low-cost health insurance for low-income New Yorkers.
eligibility: Income-based. People recently released from incarceration are eligible immediately upon release.

details.application_process: access.nyc.gov or NY State of Health marketplace.

title: The Castle — transitional housing
description: Emergency and transitional supportive housing for men with criminal-justice involvement.
eligibility: Adults with a criminal-justice history. Referrals via Fortune Society intake.

details.application_process: Call Fortune Society main line for intake screening.

title: Transitional day-labor crews
description: Same-week paid work on cleaning, grounds, and maintenance crews while CEO helps you find a permanent job.
eligibility: Released within last 6 months from a felony conviction; no current parole stay-away order against work.

details.pay_range: Daily pay at NY minimum or above
details.application_process: Walk in for orientation Mon-Fri 8am at CEO Manhattan.

title: Ready, Willing & Able
description: 9–12 month paid work + housing + training program for men with histories of incarceration or homelessness.
eligibility: Men 18+, drug- and alcohol-free, willing to commit to full program.

details.application_process: Walk in Mon-Thu 7am at The Doe Fund Harlem intake.

title: Free IT support training (15 weeks)
description: Full-time tuition-free training leading to CompTIA A+ certification and IT support placement.
eligibility: Adults 18+. Background check varies by employer partner — many are fair-chance.

details.application_process: perscholas.org/apply — info session, basic assessment, interview.

title: Record sealing & expungement help
description: Free legal assistance to seal eligible NY criminal records under CPL 160.59.
eligibility: Income-qualified New Yorkers with eligible convictions.

details.application_process: Call Legal Aid Reentry Unit for intake.

title: Career Center — job placement
description: Job readiness workshops, fair-chance employer connections, ongoing coaching.
eligibility: Adults with criminal-justice history. No fee.
